data.title: Why Are Chinese Tech Stocks Down Today? Alibaba (BABA) Leads Sell-Off
data.description:
Alibaba Group (BABA) (HK:9988), China's largest e-commerce and cloud computing company, led a drop in Chinese technology stocks after the U.S. Defense Department briefly placed several major firms on a military-linked companies list. The Chinese AI leader's stock slid more than 3% in Hong Kong, while Baidu (BIDU) (HK:9888) and BYD Co. (BYDDF) (HK:1211) were down about 1%.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The Pentagon list named Alibaba, Baidu, and BYD as companies allegedly connected to China's military. The document was later marked "unpublished" and removed without explanation, but the move still unsettled investors and kept sentiment cautious.

Alibaba denied the allegation, saying it "is not a Chinese military company nor part of any military-civil fusion strategy," and added that it would take legal action against attempts to misrepresent the business. 

What This Means for Chinese Tech Stocks

When Tencent (TCEHY) and CATL (CYATY) were added to the list in 2021, their stocks dropped quickly. There were no fines or direct penalties, but the move created fear in the market. For instance, some investment funds stepped back, banks tightened compliance checks, and a few U.S. tech partnerships slowed. It showed that being named alone can create pressure, even without formal restrictions.

That's why investors are watching Alibaba, Baidu, and BYD closely now. All three are expanding in AI, cloud, robotics, and self-driving tech, areas already linked to U.S. export rules. If they are officially added to the list, partners and investors may step back until there is more clarity.

Which Chinese Stock Is the Best Buy? 

Turning to Wall Street, all three stocks mentioned above show upside potential ahead. Alibaba has a Strong Buy consensus with about 30% upside, while BYD Company also carries a Strong Buy rating and roughly 28% upside. Baidu is likewise rated Strong Buy and offers about 24% upside.

Among them, Alibaba shows the highest expected return, though analysts remain broadly positive on all three names.
data.author: Shalu Saraf

data.title: The Week That Was, The Week Ahead: Macro and Markets, Feb. 15
data.description:
U.S. stocks ended the week lower as investors moved away from big tech and focused more on safer areas of the market. The S&P 500 (SPX) fell 1.28%, the Nasdaq 100 (NDX) dropped 1.27%, and the Dow (DJIA) slid 1.15%. At the same time, the 10-year Treasury yield eased to about 4.09%, while gold (CM:XAUUSD) jumped above $5,000 and oil (CM:CL) held near $63 a barrel.

data.title: 'I've Changed My Assumptions' – AI Is Coming for Office Work Faster Than Expected
data.description:
Anthropic's latest workplace push is adding a new layer to the AI story that investors are watching closely. Many firms have spent the past year building narrow AI tools for finance, law, and marketing. However, Anthropic is now offering a broader platform that could handle many of the same tasks in one place.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

A recent Financial Times report described how Supercell used an AI analyst agent to produce a monthly report in five minutes instead of three hours. The company's finance lead said, "I was pretty bearish before starting this. I've changed my assumptions." That example shows how quickly routine office work is already shifting.

Anthropic's new tools, released under its Claude Cowork platform, are built to support tasks like contract review, forecasting, and customer support with less human input. The company says the goal is to make "the same powerful agent, but much more accessible." As a result, investors are starting to ask whether firms still need many separate niche AI products.

AI and the Next Wave of Office Job Pressure

This news also connects with a recent warning from Microsoft's (MSFT) AI chief, Mustafa Suleyman. In an interview with the Financial Times, he said AI will begin reshaping white-collar work in the next 12 to 18 months. This timeline suggests that profound change is not distant. Instead, it may arrive quickly across banking, legal work, sales, and media.

At the same time, this does not mean every worker or firm is replaced overnight. In many cases, AI is first taking over the repetitive work that is "least interesting," as Pigment's leadership described, while humans remain responsible for judgment and oversight.

What It Means for Investors and AI Stocks

For markets, the key issue is where value will build. Large platform firms may gain more leverage if they offer broad AI systems that reduce the need for many smaller subscriptions. Anthropic's move also raises questions for firms selling narrow tools on top of the big models.

On the other hand, industry leaders argue that specialists still matter. Legal AI providers point to the need for trust, audit trails, and strong data controls. One legal executive said, "Domain-specific tools only increase in value," because regulated fields require deeper checks than a general system can offer.

As Anthropic expands and Microsoft highlights the speed of change, the next year may bring sharper shifts in how companies spend on software, productivity, and staffing.

We used TipRanks' Comparison Tool to compare notable publicly traded companies that employ chatbots similar to ChatGPT, Grok, and Claude. It's an excellent tool to gain a broader perspective on each stock and the AI industry as a whole.
data.author: Ran Melamed

data.title: Are the Markets Open Today, Washington's Birthday?
data.description:
The U.S. stock markets are closed today, February 16, 2026, in honor of Washington's Birthday.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Keep track of all stock market holidays with TipRanks' Stock Market Holidays calendar.

Will the Holiday Effect Impact the Markets This Week?

Understanding how the stock market might be affected by the holiday will help you invest well and beat the markets. Investors sometimes refer to the pre-holiday market rise and the post-holiday price drift as the "holiday effect."

The holiday effect is linked to investors' general optimism and confidence. The fact that customers tend to spend more money around the holidays is one explanation for the pre-holiday market surge. The share prices of retailers, in particular, may increase as a result.

As for the post-holiday price drift, one reason for it is that many traders and market analysts take time off work during the holidays to relax from their regular trading and investment research schedules. Those investors view the holidays as a time to unwind and refresh. Alternatively, some traders see the season as a chance to work harder and get some tactical advantages in the market, maybe by taking advantage of the period when many other traders take time off.

Read TipRanks News to learn about Washington's Birthday holiday effect on the stock market. And remember to always use TipRanks to stay up-to-date on your stock research.
data.author: TipRanks Team

data.title: 'Worst Selloff in 25 Years,' Says Dan Ives: Software Stocks NOW, CRM, MSFT in Focus
data.description:
Wedbush Securities' top analyst Daniel Ives called the recent software selloff "the worst he's witnessed in 25 years." Fears of AI disruption from Anthropic's new Claude model updates have triggered sharp declines in software stocks. Even so, Ives views the plunge in ServiceNow (NOW), Salesforce (CRM), and Microsoft (MSFT) as a "generational buying opportunity," driven by a wide gap between market prices and intrinsic value.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Ives is a five-star analyst on TipRanks, ranking #636 out of the 12,108 analysts tracked. He has a 53% success rate and an average return per rating of 13.40%.

NOW, CRM, MSFT Lose Billions

Ives argues investors wrongly assume enterprise software will become obsolete in the AI era. The stock price declines in these three stocks have particularly caught Ives' attention. Year-to-date, NOW has plummeted 30%, CRM has cratered 28.4%, and MSFT has declined 17%.

Ives believes that AI will complement existing software models rather than displace them. His view stems from a broader claim about AI integrating with and enhancing current modeling frameworks instead of rendering them obsolete.

Based on current prices, CRM now trades at a forward price-to-earnings (P/E) multiple of 16.1x, about 30% lower than the sector median of 23.2x. This is despite reporting triple-digit growth in Data Cloud and $900 million in AI ARR (annual recurring revenue) for the September quarter.

Meanwhile, ServiceNow has pursued an aggressive acquisition strategy to counter AI threats. Yet, the market is penalizing the cloud security provider amid fears that traditional Software-as-a-Service models will become obsolete.

Even tech giant Microsoft has not been spared from investor anxiety. Investors were disappointed by Microsoft Azure's 38% growth projection, dragging its shares down. The fact remains that demand exists, but Microsoft lacks extra compute capacity, which is all tied up in internal projects. Microsoft prefers to rent AI capacity to Anthropic and OpenAI to earn short-term revenue, even though they could disrupt its business in the long run.

CRM, NOW, MSFT: Which Is the Best Software Stock?

We used the TipRanks Stock Comparison Tool to determine which stock among the three is currently favored by analysts. Currently, analysts have assigned a Strong Buy consensus rating to Microsoft and ServiceNow, with NOW stock offering a higher upside potential of more than 78% over the next 12 months.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: After a 24% YTD Drop, Is Opendoor Stock (OPEN) a Bargain Buy Ahead of Q4 Earnings?
data.description:
Opendoor Technologies (OPEN) has faced a difficult start to the year, with the stock down about 24% year-to-date as investors remain concerned about housing market trends and profitability. As the company gets ready to report its first full-quarter results under new CEO Kaz Nejatian on February 19, investors are optimistic on the company's updates around the turnaround progress and early signs of recovery. That said, the stock is best suited for risk-tolerant investors.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

For context, Opendoor Technologies is a digital platform that streamlines the process of buying and selling homes.

All Eyes on Opendoor's Turnaround as Earnings Approach

OPEN stock was one of the meme stocks of the year in 2025. It has come down from those highs, as the company's iBuying model has struggled to perform in a difficult housing market. At the same time, mortgage rates have stayed high even after the Federal Reserve began cutting interest rates, although there are early signs that conditions could start improving.

This creates both challenges and opportunities for Opendoor under the new CEO Nejatian. He has already begun reshaping the company's strategy by reducing costs, increasing the use of AI, and focusing more on transaction volume. Nejatian has also pointed out that management relied too heavily on outside consultants in the past and is now bringing more of that work in-house.

Still, the turnaround may take time. Revenue continues to decline, falling 34% year over year in the third quarter. Inventory dropped from 6,288 homes to 3,319 by the end of the quarter, while home sales fell to 2,568 from 3,165 a year earlier. The company also remains unprofitable.

What's Lies Ahead for Investors?

On Wall Street, analysts expect a Q4 loss of $0.12 per share, compared with a loss of $0.16 a year ago. Meanwhile, revenue is projected to come in at $594.04 million, down from $1.1 billion in the same quarter last year.

Opendoor's new CEO has improved investor sentiment, but significant challenges remain, and consistent profitability is still uncertain. Given the stock's volatility and weak financial outlook, investors may want to proceed cautiously and carefully weigh the risks tied to the company's turnaround.

Is OPEN Stock a Good Buy?

Turning to Wall Street, analysts have a Hold consensus rating on OPEN stock based on two Holds assigned in the past three months. Furthermore, the average OPEN price target of $5.0 per share implies an upside of 12.5%.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: PLTR Stock Jumps from Sell to Buy — Analyst Says 'AI Agents Not a Threat to Palantir'
data.description:
Freedom Capital Markets analyst Almas Almaganbetov issued a rare double upgrade for Palantir Technologies (PLTR) after its robust Q4FY25 results and upbeat guidance. He upgraded PLTR from Sell to Buy, stating that he does not see AI agents as a threat to Palantir's long-term business model. Almaganbetov also set a price target of $170, implying 29.4% upside potential from current levels. PLTR stock gained 1.7% on Friday, following the upgrade.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The big data analytics company reported Q4 earnings per share of $0.25, surpassing the $0.23 consensus. Revenue hit $1.407 billion, up 70% from the prior year and ahead of the $1.34 billion forecast. Commercial revenue surged 137% year-over-year to $507 million, while government revenue grew 66% to $570 million.

Analyst Highlights AIP Growth Story

Almaganbetov noted that Palantir's Q4 results far exceeded both management's guidance and his estimates. The company's outlook for Q1 and FY26 also topped consensus. He noted that this strong forecast signals Palantir management's confidence that demand for AI infrastructure is durable and structural, not cyclical.

Almaganbetov remains particularly impressed by the growth in the company's Artificial Intelligence Platform (AIP), which is driving U.S. market growth by accelerating sales and turning pilots into major contracts. He called the recent sharp pullback "unjustified," given continued hypergrowth and the underlying strength in AIP. He also raised his revenue forecasts for fiscal 2026 and 2027, reiterating that new AI agents pose no long-term risk.

Not all analysts agree. D.A. Davidson analyst Gil Luria maintained his "Hold" rating, viewing PLTR shares as "expensive." Meanwhile, he dismissed concerns from Big Short investor Michael Burry's recent 10,000-page thesis, which raises concerns about the sustainability of the current AI investment cycle. Following his newsletter, PLTR stock plunged 6.7% on February 12.

Is Palantir a Good Stock to Buy?

Wall Street remains divided on Palantir's long-term prospects. On TipRanks, PLTR stock has a Moderate Buy consensus rating based on 11 Buys, six Holds, and two Sell ratings. The average Palantir Technologies price target of $192.38 implies 46.4% upside potential from current levels. Over the past year, PLTR shares have gained 10.3%.
data.author: Sheryl Sheth

data.title: OpenAI Hires OpenClaw Creator Steinberger to Power AI Agent Push
data.description:
OpenAI is doubling down on its push into autonomous AI agents with a key new hire. The company has tapped Peter Steinberger, creator of the open-source AI agent OpenClaw, as part of its efforts to expand its product capabilities. His appointment follows OpenClaw's recent surge in popularity, driven by its ability to function as a personal assistant.

President's Day Sale - 70% Off

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

According to a social media update from OpenAI's CEO Sam Altman, OpenClaw will remain an open-source project under a foundation, with OpenAI continuing to support its development.

In a separate post, Steinberger said he is joining OpenAI to be part of the "frontier of AI research and development" while continuing to build new technologies. He emphasized that keeping OpenClaw open source has always been a priority for him.

OpenAI Strengthens AI Agent Push

The move reflects the rising importance of AI agents. Notably, Altman believes agents will shape the next phase of AI, even though the technology is still in its early stages. As a result, tech companies are now competing to build smarter systems that can complete real-world tasks with greater independence.

OpenClaw, previously known as Clawdbot and Moltbot, has gained a strong following since its launch in November thanks to its ability to operate autonomously. The tool can manage tasks such as clearing email inboxes, booking restaurant reservations, and checking users in for flights. It also integrates with messaging platforms like Meta's (META) WhatsApp and Slack, allowing users to control the agent directly through those apps.

Meanwhile, OpenClaw has also raised concerns about possible security risks if used incorrectly.
